Title: The PcMYB44-mediated miR397-PcLACs module regulates defence-induced lignification in pear resistance to fungal disease

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell walls and contributes to defence against fungal pathogen invasion
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Multicopper oxidase with three Cu-oxidase domains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively polymerizes monolignols into lignin in the cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated after Botryosphaeria dothidea and Alternaria alternata infection; peaks around 72 hpi
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated after pathogen infection; induced in leaves/fruits when PcmiR397 is silenced
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignification and enhances resistance to A. alternata and B. dothidea when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcLAC2 is a validated target of PcmiR397. 2. Its transcript accumulates inversely with PcmiR397 during fungal infection. 3. Transient overexpression in pear leaves reduced disease progression and fungal biomass. 4. It slightly increased lignin content, supporting a role in defence-induced lignification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 5′ RLM-RACE; N. benthamiana co-transformation with target-site GFP reporter; transient overexpression in pear leaves; RT-qPCR; fungal biomass quantification; lignin measurement
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Infection with Alternaria alternata or Botryosphaeria dothidea
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering broad-spectrum fungal resistance via enhanced lignification; may be stacked with PcLAC4/PcLAC7 or combined with PcmiR397 suppression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of multiple PcLACs regulated by PcmiR397; lignin increase upon overexpression was slight and not significant in the reported assay

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell walls and contributes to defence against fungal pathogen invasion
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Multicopper oxidase with three Cu-oxidase domains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively polymerizes monolignols into lignin in the cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated after Botryosphaeria dothidea and Alternaria alternata infection; peaks around 72 hpi
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated after pathogen infection and after PcmiR397 silencing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin accumulation and enhances resistance to A. alternata and B. dothidea when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcLAC4 is a direct cleavage target of PcmiR397 validated by 5′ RLM-RACE and co-expression assays. 2. PcLAC4 localizes to the cell wall, matching the site of lignin deposition. 3. Its expression is induced by fungal infection and inversely correlated with PcmiR397. 4. Transient overexpression decreased lesion severity and fungal biomass and increased lignin content in pear leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 5′ RLM-RACE; N. benthamiana co-transformation; western blot of GFP fusion reporter; transient overexpression in pear leaves; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; lignin measurement; fungal biomass quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Infection with Alternaria alternata or Botryosphaeria dothidea
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for improving pear fungal resistance by increasing defence-induced lignification; suitable for overexpression or release from PcmiR397 repression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PcLAC4 overexpression reduced A. alternata and B. dothidea biomass to about 1/2 and 1/15 of controls, respectively

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell walls and contributes to defence against fungal pathogen invasion
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Multicopper oxidase with three Cu-oxidase domains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively polymerizes monolignols into lignin in the cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly up-regulated after Botryosphaeria dothidea and Alternaria alternata infection; peaks around 72 hpi
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated after pathogen infection and after PcmiR397 silencing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin accumulation and enhances resistance to A. alternata and B. dothidea when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcLAC7 is a validated target of PcmiR397. 2. Its transcript shows an inverse pattern relative to PcmiR397 during pathogen infection. 3. Overexpression reduced disease progression and fungal biomass. 4. Overexpression markedly increased lignin content, supporting a role in defence-induced lignification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana co-transformation with target-site GFP reporter; transient overexpression in pear leaves; RT-qPCR; fungal biomass quantification; lignin measurement
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Infection with Alternaria alternata or Botryosphaeria dothidea
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering pathogen resistance through enhanced lignification; potentially valuable in gene stacking with PcLAC4 and PcmiR397 suppression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note PcLAC7 responded especially strongly to pathogen infection and may also be regulated at the promoter level beyond miR397

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell walls and contributes to defence against fungal pathogen invasion
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Defence-induced lignification via transcriptional control of the miR397-LAC module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses PcMIR397 transcription, thereby indirectly increasing PcLAC2; PcLAC4; PcLAC7 expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fungal pathogen infection; jasmonic acid; salicylic acid; hydrogen peroxide/defence signals
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as an upstream inducer of lignification by relieving miR397-mediated repression of laccases during pathogen attack
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible defence-signal-linked feedforward module inferred; explicit feedback loop not established
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced at 1 and 3 dpi after Botryosphaeria dothidea or Alternaria alternata infection; also induced by JA, SA, and H2O2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes defence-induced lignification and pear resistance indirectly by repressing PcmiR397
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcMYB44 binds both PcMIR397 promoters in Y1H assays. 2. PcMYB44 represses PcMIR397 promoter activity in dual-luciferase assays, dependent on the predicted binding motif. 3. PcMYB44 is nuclear localized and induced by fungal infection and defence-related signals. 4. PcMYB44 functions upstream of the miR397-PcLAC module to promote lignification-associated disease resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for effector constructs in dual-luciferase assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase reporter assay; promoter motif deletion assay; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; phylogenetic analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Infection with Alternaria alternata or Botryosphaeria dothidea; jasmonic acid; salicylic acid; hydrogen peroxide
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising upstream regulator for breeding or engineering broad-spectrum fungal resistance by tuning the miR397-LAC lignification module; caution that broader defence/hormone signaling effects may occur
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PcMYB86-like also repressed pMIR397-1039 in reporter assays, but was down-regulated during infection and was not concluded to be the main regulator in the response

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Conference; Jinshui No. 2 for fruit transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell walls and contributes to defence against fungal pathogen invasion
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Monolignol polymerization/lignification through post-transcriptional repression of laccases
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PcLAC2; PcLAC4; PcLAC7; additional predicted PcLACs including PcLAC11
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fungal pathogen infection; PcMYB44-mediated promoter repression; jasmonic acid; salicylic acid; hydrogen peroxide-associated defence signals
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator of defence-induced lignification; its down-regulation during infection permits PcLAC accumulation and lignin synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Down-regulated at 24–72 hpi after A. alternata or B. dothidea infection; precursor transcripts also reduced
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced during pathogen infection; increased in OE-PcMIR397 fruits and strongly decreased in STTM-PcmiR397 fruits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses lignification-associated resistance; silencing increases resistance to A. alternata and B. dothidea via up-regulation of PcLACs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcmiR397 directly cleaves multiple PcLAC transcripts, including PcLAC4, confirmed by 5′ RLM-RACE and co-transformation assays. 2. PcmiR397 and its precursor are transcriptionally repressed by pathogen infection. 3. Silencing PcmiR397 up-regulates PcLAC genes and reduces lesion size after fungal inoculation. 4. PcmiR397 is repressed upstream by PcMYB44, forming a regulatory module controlling lignification.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; STTM silencing construct driven by CaMV 35S
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Northern blot; stem-loop RT-qPCR; 5′ RLM-RACE; N. benthamiana co-transformation; transient overexpression and STTM silencing in pear fruits; lesion measurement
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Infection with Alternaria alternata or Botryosphaeria dothidea; JA; SA; H2O2
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression caused slightly larger lesions but not significantly; possible compensatory defence feedback mechanisms discussed
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent target for genome editing, promoter tuning, or STTM-based suppression to enhance broad-spectrum fungal resistance through increased lignification
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two precursor loci were identified, PcMIR397-1.0 and PcMIR397-1039; pMIR397-1039 showed stronger promoter activity and pathogen-responsive repression
